name: Pico La Capía and Monte Cueto Loop
name_original: Pico La Capía – circular desde Torrelavega por el Collado de Navardoyos
description: Hike the 8.6-mile Pico La Capía circular route from Torrelavega, gaining 1750 feet for panoramic views from Pico Dobra.
region_intro: Hiking around Torrelavega offers access to a variety of natural landscapes within Cantabria. The region features diverse terrain, ranging from coastal hills to the foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains, providing varied elevations for hiking. Natural areas such as La Viesca and the Vía Verde del Besaya offer accessible paths, while nearby mountains present more challenging routes. This combination of features makes Torrelavega a base for exploring green valleys and varied natural environments. Best hiking trails around Torrelavega The most popular hiking route is Pico La Capía and Monte Cueto Loop, an 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views from higher elevations. Another top favourite among local hikers is Viérnoles - circular from Torrelavega, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) path. This trail provides a gentle walk through the immediate surroundings of Torrelavega. Local hikers also love the Monte Dobra – Monte Cueto loop from Viérnoles, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with moderate ascents, often completed in about 3 hours 51 minutes. Hiking around Torrelavega is defined by green valleys, coastal hills, and access to the Cantabrian Mountains. The network of hiking trails offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Torrelavega are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Torrelavega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Torrelavega?
A: There are over 60 hiking routes available around Torrelavega, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, with approximately 30 easy trails, 30 moderate trails, and a few more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly walks near Torrelavega?
A: Yes, Torrelavega offers several easy and family-friendly walks. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001227107/viernoles-circular-from-torrelavega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viérnoles - circular from Torrelavega&lt;/a&gt;, a gentle 5 km path that takes about 1 hour and 20 minutes. The Vía Verde del Besaya also provides accessible and scenic routes suitable for all ages.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Torrelavega?
A: The terrain around Torrelavega is quite diverse. You can expect green valleys, coastal hills, and access to the foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. While some routes are relatively flat, like sections of the Vía Verde del Besaya, others, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001232497/pico-la-capia-and-monte-cueto-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico La Capía and Monte Cueto Loop&lt;/a&gt;, involve significant elevation gains, offering varied experiences from gentle strolls to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Torrelavega area?
A: Yes, many routes around Torrelavega are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001227107/viernoles-circular-from-torrelavega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viérnoles - circular from Torrelavega&lt;/a&gt; for an easy option, or the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Dobra – Monte Cueto loop from Viérnoles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or scenic spots accessible by hiking near Torrelavega?
A: For impressive panoramic views, consider hiking towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1511383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Ballota&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4370882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Pico La Capía (Pico Dobra)&lt;/a&gt;, reached via routes like the Pico La Capía and Monte Cueto Loop, also offers expansive vistas of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Torrelavega?
A: The hiking trails around Torrelavega are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from green valleys to coastal hills, and the accessibility to both easy walks and more challenging mountain routes.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or landmarks to explore near Torrelavega's hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The Area Natural de Especial Interés de La Viesca is a highly valued natural spot for tranquil walks. Additionally, you can find beautiful coastal highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Justa Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3688018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Sable Beach&lt;/a&gt; within reach, offering a different natural experience after your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking around Torrelavega?
A: Cantabria, where Torrelavega is located, is known for its green landscapes. While hiking is possible year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially inland, but coastal breezes can make seaside walks enjoyable. Winter hiking is also possible, though higher elevations may experience colder conditions.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, Torrelavega's strategic location means you're close to significant historical sites. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/360503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana del Mar Historic Town Centre&lt;/a&gt; is a must-visit, and you can also find beautiful religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/962386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Santa Juliana&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14994007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón de Cartes – Hermitage of San Roque loop from Altamira&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate historical elements directly into the trail.

Q: Can I access more challenging mountain hikes from Torrelavega?
A: While Torrelavega itself is not directly in the high mountains, its location provides excellent access to the broader Cantabrian Mountains. For experienced hikers seeking panoramic views and significant elevation gain, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001232497/pico-la-capia-and-monte-cueto-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico La Capía and Monte Cueto Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer a challenging experience. Furthermore, the renowned Picos de Europa National Park, with its majestic mountains, is a major hiking destination accessible from the region.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options or pilgrimage routes nearby?
A: For those interested in long-distance trekking, Torrelavega is near sections of the Camino del Norte, a historic pilgrimage route. This route offers paths along the northern Spanish coast, providing an opportunity for multi-day hikes.
tips:
Several trails converge at this pass to visit the area, near the La Fuente Las Palomas recreational area (with benches, a rehabilitated fountain and the Las Palomas sinkhole).
It has beautiful views, including a quarry looking straight ahead.
A very interesting and familiar place. I tried to get to the Sumidero de las Palomas, but it was impossible for me to get there, I would have needed not some walking sticks, but a machete!

Cima Herbosa without any type of sign and modest height (522 m.), but which has very good views, including that of La Capía Peak, of which Cueto can be said to be an antecedent and reveals the almost perfect cut of the profile before our sight.

​The Pico La Capía (also known as Pico Dobra) is a prominent rock of 606 m. altitude, with excellent views as it is located on the divide between the Besaya and Pas rivers, marking the boundary between three municipalities of Cantabria: Puente Viesgo, San Felices de Buelna and Torrelavega.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: This circular route starts from Torrelavega. While specific parking details for the exact trailhead in Torrelavega are not provided, as a major town, Torrelavega offers various parking options. It's advisable to check local parking facilities before your visit.

faqs.question: How can I get to the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: As the route begins in Torrelavega, a well-connected town in Cantabria, it is accessible via public transport. You can likely reach Torrelavega by bus or train from larger cities in the region. From there, you would need to navigate to the specific starting point of the circular hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, hiking trails in natural areas like this are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Please be mindful of any local regulations regarding dogs in the Cantabrian region.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Pico La Capía circular route can be enjoyed during most seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is recommended. Winter hikes are possible but may require appropriate gear for colder conditions and potentially slippery paths.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Pico La Capía circular route. Access to natural areas in this region of Cantabria is typically free.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Pico La Capía circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail features mostly accessible paths. Hikers can expect typical mountain and valley landscapes, likely including a mix of trails through vegetation and potentially some rocky sections as you approach the summit of Pico La Capía. Good footwear is recommended for varied surfaces.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from Pico La Capía?
faqs.answer: From the summit of Pico La Capía (Pico Dobra), you can expect excellent and unbeatable panoramic views of the surrounding Cantabrian region. The peak's strategic location on the divide between the Besaya and Pas rivers offers expansive vistas.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the Collado de Naranjos mountain pass, the Fuente de las Palomas Recreation Area, and the summits of Monte Cueto and Pico La Capía (Pico Dobra), which offers rewarding scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities along the route?
faqs.answer: The Fuente de las Palomas Recreation Area is noted as a facility along the route. For more extensive amenities like cafes or shops, you would likely need to visit Torrelavega or other nearby towns.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Sendero del Cueto, Sendero de la Capia, Sendero de la Sierra, Sendero de las Fuentes, La Montaña, Sendero de las Palomas, and Urbanización Parque de Tanos.

faqs.question: Is this hike suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, this hike requires good fitness and sure-footedness. While not explicitly labeled as 'family-friendly', older children with hiking experience and good stamina might manage it. For younger children, the distance and elevation gain might be challenging.
